subject: Physics
solution:
Let velocity of centre of mass of sphere be v.

Total kinetic energy of solid sphere is KE=12mv2+12Iω2, where, I is moment of inertia of the solid sphere about the axis passing through it's centre of mass and ω=vR pure rolling condition.

Therefore,

 7×10-3=12×1×(v)2+1225(1)R2vR2

⇒12v275=7×10-3

⇒v2=10-2

⇒v=10-1=0.1 m s-1=10 cm s-1

subject: Physics
solution:
For a circular disc made of material with density $\rho$, moment of inertia is $I = \frac{1}{2}MR^2 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R^2 T \cdot R^2 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R^4 T$.
For disc 1: $I_1 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R_1^4 T_1$
For disc 2: $I_2 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R_2^4 T_2$
Given $I_1 = I_2$:
$R_1^4 T_1 = R_2^4 T_2$
With $\frac{R_1}{R_2} = 2$:
$(2R_2)^4 T_1 = R_2^4 T_2$
$16 R_2^4 T_1 = R_2^4 T_2$
$\frac{T_1}{T_2} = \frac{1}{16}$
Therefore $\alpha = 16$.

subject: Physics
solution:
Rotational kinetic energy is given by E=12Iω2, here, I is moment of inertia of rod about an axis through its centre and perpendicular to it.

So, E=12ml212ω2

⇒E=12dAl312ω2    ∵mass=density×volume

⇒E=dA(2)324ω2

⇒ω=3EdA

Thus, the value of α=3.

subject: Physics
solution:
Let the string makes angle θ with the vertical. Weight will be balanced by the vertical component of tension, which is Tcosθ=mg and the horizontal component towards the centre will provide the required centripetal force for circular motion. Therefore,

Tsinθ=mω2lsinθ, where l=length of the string.

⇒400sinθ=0.5ω2×0.5sinθ

⇒ω=40 rad s-1

subject: Physics
solution:
CM from mass $m$: $x_{cm} = \frac{2md}{3m} = \frac{2d}{3}$.
Distances: $m$ is $2d/3$ from CM, $2m$ is $d/3$ from CM.
$I_{cm} = m(2d/3)^2 + 2m(d/3)^2 = \frac{4md^2}{9} + \frac{2md^2}{9} = \frac{2md^2}{3}$.
$L = I\omega \Rightarrow \omega = \frac{L}{I} = \frac{3L}{2md^2}$.

subject: Physics
solution:
The moment of inertia of a sphere is given by the formula I=25MR2.

Since there are two masses, the total moment of inertia about the axis perpendicular to the rod passing through the centre is 

I=2×25MR2+Ml2+r2   ...(i)

The given data is 

M=2 kgl=0.4-0.2 m=0.2 mR=0.1 m

Substituting the values in equation (i)

I=45(2×0.12)+2×2(0.22+0.1)2  kg m2⇒I=45×0.02+4×0.04  kg m2⇒I=0.016+0.16  kg m2=0.176 kg m2

solution: $\begin{aligned} \tau & =\dot{r} \times \dot{F} \\ \tau & =\left|\begin{array}{lll}\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & 1 & 2\end{array}\right| \\ \tau & =\hat{i}(2-1)-\hat{j}[0]+\hat{k}(1-2) \\ & =\hat{i}-\hat{k}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
It is given that the force F→=-Pk^

The coordinates of the origin is (0,0).

The coordinates of the point given is  (2,-3)

The displacement vector is 

r→=(0-2)i^+(0+3)j^=-2i^+3j^

Hence, the torque is 

τ→=r→×F→=(-2i^+3j^)×(-Pk^)

⇒τ→=-2Pj^-3Pi^=P(ai^+bj^)

So, it can be written

a=-3, b=-2

∴  ab=32⇒ so x=3

subject: Physics
solution:
Given $\theta = \dfrac{5t^4}{40} - \dfrac{t^3}{3} = \dfrac{t^4}{8} - \dfrac{t^3}{3}$

Angular velocity $\omega = \dfrac{d\theta}{dt} = \dfrac{4t^3}{8} - \dfrac{3t^2}{3} = \dfrac{t^3}{2} - t^2$

Angular acceleration $\alpha = \dfrac{d\omega}{dt} = \dfrac{3t^2}{2} - 2t$

At $t = 10$ s, $\alpha = \dfrac{3(10)^2}{2} - 2(10)$

$\alpha = 150 - 20 = 130$ rad/s$^2$

Answer: $130$

subject: Physics
solution:
Let the angular speed of disc be ω.

Using conservation of mechanical energy

mg2R=12Idisc ω2+12Iparticle ω2

Where, I is moment of inertia.

⇒mg2R=ω22m22+mR2

⇒mg2R=ω2232mR2

⇒34ω2=2gR             

 ⇒ω2=8g3R

Thus, angular speed is ω=803R rad s-1

Given ω=4x3R

Comparing both, 16x3R=803R

Therefore, the value of x=5.

subject: Physics
solution:
For solid cylinder: $I = \frac{1}{2}mR^2$
Large cylinder: mass $m_1 = \rho \pi R^2 L$, so $I_1 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R^2 L \times R^2 = \frac{\rho \pi R^4 L}{2}$
Small cylinder (carved out): length L/2, radius R/3, mass $m_2 = \rho \pi(R/3)^2(L/2) = \frac{\rho \pi R^2 L}{18}$
$I_2 = \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{\rho \pi R^2 L}{18} \times (R/3)^2 = \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{\rho \pi R^2 L}{18} \times \frac{R^2}{9} = \frac{\rho \pi R^4 L}{324}$
$\frac{I_1}{I_2} = \frac{\rho \pi R^4 L/2}{\rho \pi R^4 L/324} = \frac{324}{2} = 162$

subject: Physics
solution:
The formula to calculate the moment of inertia I of the cylinder about its radial axis is given by

I=Mr2   ...1

Also, the torque τ on the cylinder about the central axis because of the application of the external force is given by

τ= Fr   ...2

Also, the torque can be expressed as

τ=Iα   ...3

Substitute the expressions from equation (1) and (2) into equation (3) and simplify to obtain the angular acceleration.

Fr= Mr2α⇒α= FMr   ...4

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (4) to calculate the required angular acceleration.

α= 52.5 N5 kg×0.70 m= 15 rad s-2
